title: physocarpus opulifolius var. intermedius
intro: Physocarpus opulifolius var. intermedius, also known as the 'Dart's Gold' or 'Golden Ninebark', is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which is also found in willow bark. The medicinal properties of Physocarpus opulifolius var. intermedius have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, offering relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to promote overall well-being and reduce stress.
